description: An edge or point is 'tiso' when it is fine enough to cut or pierce readily. The adjective extends to perception or thought that detects distinctions keenly. The word 'kiro' (precise) concerns exactness against a standard; sharp perception may still reach an imprecise conclusion.
articulatory_notes: The tongue tip closes against the upper teeth for dental 't' and releases into high-front 'i'. A narrow groove along the tongue directs voiceless 's' before the lips round for mid-back 'o'. Stress falls on the first syllable.
sound_symbolism: The clean dental release into high 'i' and the narrow hiss of 's' give 'tiso' a crisp, fine-edged contour. That contour offers a mnemonic for a keen edge.
usage_notes: The phrase 'tiso kiru' means a sharp chisel. Established figurative uses include 'tiso remo' (sharp thought) and 'tiso mirae' (sharp eye).

semantic_domains.cognition: keen attention or thought that detects fine distinctions
semantic_domains.physical: a fine edge or point able to cut or pierce readily
